title: Stoned Dates Middle Eastern Pilaf
description: A buttery, fluffy middle eastern pilaf featuring stoned dates and a touch of fermenting for an egg-free-friendly dish perfect for weeknight meals.

ingredients.name: 15 dates, pitted and roughly chopped (fermented for at least 48 hours)

instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water. Soak it in 2 cups of water for at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Drain the water from the rice, then heat a large skillet over medium-high heat with half of the butter and sauté the minced garlic until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the drained rice to the skillet and toast it in the butter for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. The goal is to toast the rice, not brown it; this helps develop a nutty flavor.

Pour in the water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer gently until all the liquid has been absorbed by the rice (about 20 minutes for regular rice or slightly longer for long-grain basmati).
In a small bowl, combine remaining butter, cumin, salt, pepper, and roughly chopped dates. Stir this mixture into the cooked rice just before serving.
Serve warm as an accompaniment to grilled meats, roasted vegetables, or alongside a salad.
